channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Thought My System Was Secure… Until I Found This One Mistake
item.description: I used to think my system was secure. Not “enterprise-grade security team” secure. But definitely: 👉 “I know what I’m doing” secure. Strong passwords? ✅ Firewall enabled? ✅ Antivirus installed? ✅...

item.title: What Diction Does When Transcription Fails
item.description: You're mid-sentence. Something goes wrong -- bad connection, phone locks, app gets suspended. The keyboard goes quiet. The transcription never comes back. Most voice keyboards treat that as an...

item.title: Claude Code used 2.5M tokens on my project. I got it down to 425K with 6 hook scripts.
item.description: My team and I have been using Claude Code as our primary coding agent for about six months now. Somewhere around last month, I started hitting limits on my MAX plans. My prompts weren't unusually...

item.title: How I Built a Simple Niche Site System Using Hugo, AI, and Pinterest
item.description: Most people overcomplicate building niche websites. I did the same at the beginning — trying different platforms, plugins, and setups. It felt messy and slow. At some point, I decided to simplify...

item.title: HTML Contact Forms Don't Send Email (Here’s What Actually Does)
item.description: HTML forms don’t send email. They never did. Here’s what’s actually going on and how to build one that works. If you've ever written a contact form in HTML and expected it to send an email, you've...

item.title: How to Optimize Amazon S3 Images via ShortPixel Global CDN (Serve Faster & Reduce Costs)
item.description: If you store images in Amazon S3, you're probably paying more than you need to for bandwidth. Every time a visitor requests an image, S3 serves the original file — full resolution, uncompressed,...

item.title: I Built a Free Clearbit Alternative — Company Enrichment API for Developers
item.description: Clearbit killed their free tier in April 2025. Now it's "Breeze Intelligence" — $75/month minimum, HubSpot subscription required, credits expire monthly. I needed company enrichment for a side...

item.title: Red Team vs Blue Team — I Choose You!
item.description: It’s 1999. You slide a Pokémon Red Version cartridge into your Game Boy. Four AA batteries, a notebook full of scribbles, and a dream: beat the Elite Four. But this isn’t just a Pokémon...

item.title: I built a 1.7kb VDOM-less framework, it went "viral", and Reddit banned me 🤣
item.description: Have you ever spent days crafting an open-source project, finally launched it, watched it get 20K views in just a few hours... and then had a robot permanently ban your account? Well, that was my...

item.title: Designing a Hardware-Bound License Server for Embedded Linux Devices (Part 1: Server Architecture)
item.description: This post walks through the server-side architecture of a licensing system we built for ARM64 embedded Linux devices that uses TPM 2.0 chips as hardware trust anchors. The system issues licenses...

item.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027,...

item.title: A new Nintendo Switch 2 could be the poster child for replaceable batteries
item.description: Nintendo plans to release a Switch 2 revision in the European Union that will let users replace their own batteries, Nikkei reports. The current version of the Switch 2 has a glued-in battery. But...

item.title: Nvidia has an OpenClaw strategy. Do you?
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027,...

item.title: Future Sony PlayStation games will use AI to imagine new frames
item.description: Mark Cerny, the lead architect of the PlayStation 5 and PS5 Pro, told Digital Foundry that ML-based frame generation tech is coming to "PlayStation platforms" in the future, letting the game...

item.title: Build a Domain-Specific Embedding Model in Under a Day
Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either
item.description: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.

item.title: Windows 11 is finally getting a movable taskbar
item.description: It's taken almost five years, but Microsoft is finally bringing back the ability to move the Windows taskbar to the top or sides of your screen. The movable taskbar is part of some much bigger...

item.title: Microsoft is ending the Windows Update nightmare — and letting you pause them indefinitely
item.description: In 2015, Microsoft decided that you shouldn't be in control of updating your PC anymore. At first, it seemed like a good idea to keep malware at bay - but soon, users discovered their computers...

item.title: This is Microsoft’s plan to fix Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft has faced a breakdown of trust in Windows 11 and a backlash over AI additions to its operating system in recent months. After promising to rebuild trust in Windows earlier this year,...

item.title: Trump takes another shot at dismantling state AI regulation
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday unveiled its new legislative blueprint for AI regulation, and the seven-point plan includes a clear message: The federal government should avoid many AI...

item.title: These electronic Polaroids are powered by your phone’s NFC chip
item.description: E Ink displays have expanded beyond e-readers. They're now used in digital photo frames, smartphones, and even the price tags found on grocery store shelves. VidaBay's Classic Plus NFC E-Ink...

item.title: Trivy Security Scanner GitHub Actions Breached, 75 Tags Hijacked to Steal CI/CD Secrets
item.description: Trivy, a popular open-source vulnerability scanner maintained by Aqua Security, was compromised a second time within the span of a month to deliver malware that stole sensitive CI/CD secrets. The...

item.title: Nine months later, the Trump phone still doesn’t exist
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. Once again, we're waiting for a response. It's...

item.title: Anker’s Qi2 MagGo Power Bank is down to its best price in months
item.description: Given how often most of us rely on our smartphones, it’s a good idea to keep a power bank on hand in the event your phone dies while you’re out and about. If you don’t own one already, Anker’s...

item.title: WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more
item.description: New AI agents on WordPress.com could lower barriers to publishing while increasing machine-generated content across the web.

item.title: Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
item.description: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

item.title: Google’s battery-powered Nest Doorbell is $40 off right now
item.description: Buying a video doorbell is worth it for several reasons. They’re convenient if you get a lot of deliveries or visitors, as they let you glance at a phone alert to decide whether it’s necessary to...

item.title: Critical Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-33017 Triggers Attacks within 20 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A critical security flaw impacting Langflow has come under active exploitation within 20 hours of public disclosure, highlighting the speed at which threat actors weaponize newly published...

item.title: At Palantir’s Developer Conference, AI Is Built to Win Wars
item.description: As business soars, Palantir is doubling down on a vision of AI built for battlefield advantage—and attracting customers who agree.

item.title: What's New in Mellea 0.4.0 + Granite Libraries Release
